Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria with ketonuria and glucosuria, but low HbA1c, suggesting possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction rather than diabetes. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has abnormal odor, but no evidence of infection. Findings are consistent with renal impairment and possible metabolic acidosis; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
